title: Sed injection in organs/hand.sh edit command

fix_summary.vulnerability: Sed command injection in organs/hand.sh edit command
fix_summary.root_cause: Prior implementation used sed with user-controlled OLD and NEW parameters without proper escaping, allowing shell metacharacters and sed commands to break out of the s command and inject arbitrary sed commands
fix_summary.fix_applied: Replaced sed-based replacement with Python str.replace() - a literal string replacement that is immune to sed injection attacks
fix_summary.implementation_details: Embedded Python script uses file I/O and atomic replacement (tempfile + os.replace) to prevent injection while maintaining POSIX compliance

AC-001.description: Pipe characters in OLD/NEW are properly escaped
AC-001.evidence: Pipe character replaced literally without sed command interpretation

AC-002.description: Ampersand backreference in replacement is escaped
AC-002.evidence: Ampersand treated as literal character, not expanded as backreference

AC-003.description: Regex metacharacters are treated as literals
AC-003.evidence: Dot, brackets, anchors all treated as literal strings

AC-004.evidence: Malicious payloads treated as literal strings, no command execution

AC-005.evidence: All normal edits work correctly with Python str.replace()

AC-006.evidence: Backup file created with timestamp before edit

AC-007.evidence: Proper error messages and exit codes for non-existent files

code_quality.implementation: Python str.replace() for literal string replacement
strengths: Immune to sed injection (no regex interpretation)
code_quality.notes: Implementation is significantly more secure than escaping approach because it eliminates the sed interpreter entirely for replacement logic

tester_signature: chamu (QA Lead) — Jit Oracle Multiagent System
